free_text: proton-, antiproton-, pion-, kaon- and deuteron-induced
spallation reactions on thin and thick targets.
Thin Ag, Ho, Au and Pb target were investigated as well
as thick Pb and depleted U-targets. The Pb targets had
The U-targets, 8 cm in diameter and 40 cm long, were
The longest Pb and U-targets that fit in the bnb, l=40-
cm, correspond to about 2.1, 3.5 reaction lengths or
equivalently to a reaction probability of 88% or 97% in
Pb or U, respectively. It should also be mentioned that
the U-target, because of its high intrinsic
radioactivity, had to be shielded inside the bnb
scattering chamber. The lateral shielding was made of
The 4pi-neutron detector consists of a spherical
vessel with outer and inner diameters of 140 and 40-cm,
respectively. This 50 cm thick vessel is filled with
1500 liters of scintillator liquid loaded with
Two thin, 2mm, scintillator detectors and over a
distance of 16 m.  In addition to the detectors, a
system of five veto counters, were used to reject
particles traveling off axis and/or hitting the neutron
detector during the neutron counting gate. Additional
detector was used to focus the beam through "Berlin
respectively, on pions plus lighter particles and
The measured neutron multiplicity distribution has to
be corrected for detection efficiency, dead time,
uncorrelated background as measured with no target has
been subtracted.  The correlated background has been
measured on line in a second, 45 microseconds long
gate, issued 400 microseconds after the first counting
gate and has been corrected for by deconvolution.

- By authors:  the mean neutron multiplicity of
antiproton+lead on thick target is considerably
larger than for proton+lead if compared at the same
incident kinetic energy.  The available incident energy
for antiprotons is, however, given by kinetic energy
Performing the comparison at the same incident
available energy we find that the mean neutron
multiplicity of proton and antiproton induced reactions
are very similar for thick target.
